### Post by mathjazz on 2005-02-19
Colours on my desktop image look very bad. Look at the picture attached.

I also experience slow "logout animation"; the picture becomes darker in only three steps, so the animation is not smooth at all.

I have an IBM P76 monitor and S3 Savage 4 graphics adapter.

Here's my /etc/X11/XF86Config-4:
```

miha@ThinkServus:/etc/X11 $ cat XF86Config-4

EndSection

EndSection

Section "Screen"

### Post by jeremy on 2005-02-19
To change the colour of your desktop simply right click anywhere on the desktop (on the brown), then click 'Change desktop background', at the bottom of the new window, under 'Desktop colours' you will be able to chose a colour you like.

---

### Post by kassetra on 2005-02-19
mathjazz: that is the default setup for ubuntu, the chocolate brown desktop.
 You can change the desktop background by going:
 Computer -> Desktop Preferences -> Desktop Background
 
 If you would like more backgrounds or different themes, here are some places to help you find a look and feel you would prefer:
 [gnome-look]("http://gnome-look.org/")
 [ubuntuforums art section]("http://www.ubuntuforums.org/forumdisplay.php?f=16")
 [art.gnome.org]("http://art.gnome.org")
 
 I hope that helps :)

### Post by kassetra on 2005-02-22
OH!  Your resolution is set for something like 256 colors!

This is an easy enough fix:
(I'm assuming you're running warty)

1. Open a terminal and type this command:
 ```
sudo cp /etc/X11/XF68Config-4 /etc/X11/XF86Config-4.0
``` 
2. Now type this command:
 ```
sudo gedit /etc/X11/XF86Config-4
``` 
3. When the text editor pops up, scroll down until you see a line that says:
"Section "Screen"
4. Now, inside of that Section, look for a line that says "DefaultDepth"
5. It probably says something like 8 or 16 just to the right of "DefaultDepth"...
6. If you look just under where it says "DefaultDepth" you'll see a bunch of numbers... the numbers that say something like 1280x1024 are resolution sizes, but the numbers like 1, 4, 8, 15, 16, 24 on a line all by themselves are the number of colors available for your screen (the "Depth" of the screen) ... Find the highest number on a line all by itself in this section (mine says 24)
7. You'll want to change the "DefaultDepth" number to the highest number you found in the step before.
8. Save, Close, and reboot your computer.
9. [color=Red]IF YOUR COMPUTER WILL NOT LET YOU IN WHEN IT COMES UP:
 LOG IN AS YOU IN THE TERMINAL USE THIS COMMAND TO FIX IT AND REBOOT AGAIN:[/color]
 ```
**sudo cp /etc/X11/XF86Config-4.0 /etc/X11/XF86Config-4**
``` 

and then come back here and let me know.

---

### Post by mathjazz on 2005-02-25
You are not going to believe it.

It already was set to 24, but I set it to 16 and now it works great. ;)

Thanks!

But gnome logout animation is still not smooth ...

---
